content:
# Acts Chapter 8 — Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Chapter:** Acts 8 (Philip in Samaria, Simon the Magician, Ethiopian Eunuch)

| Verse | Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Resolution |
|-------|-------|------|-------|--------|-------------------|
| 1 | Tone of persecution | "came-about ... a great persecution" | "there arose ... a great persecution" | "a brutal wave of persecution crashed down" | Follows LOGOS; PNEUMA adds intensity not in the Greek |
| 4 | Proclamation verb | "proclaiming-good-news [with] the word" | "proclaiming the word" | "announcing the good news of the word" | "proclaiming the good news of the word" -- the Greek euangelizomenoi requires the "good news" element |
| 5 | "Christ" vs. "Messiah" | "the Christ" | "the Christ" | "the Messiah" | "the Christ" -- 2-to-1 agreement, preserves Greek form |
| 5 | "proclaiming" vs. "preaching" | "was proclaiming" (kerusson) | "was preaching" (kerusson) | "began proclaiming" | "was proclaiming" -- LOGOS's own note #12 distinguishes kerusson from euangelizomenos |
| 7 | Syntax of exorcisms | Preserves anacoluthon | Smooths syntax | Smooths syntax | Follows LOGOS's smoothed reading for clarity while keeping close to Greek |
| 8 | "great joy" rendering | "great joy" | "great joy" | "explosion of joy" | "great joy" -- literal Greek; PNEUMA's metaphor is interpretive |
| 10 | Title phrasing | "the Power of God, the [one] being called Great" | "the power of God which is called Great" | "the Power of God, the one called Great" | "the Power of God, the one called Great" -- preserves the title with EMET/PNEUMA's phrasing |
| 13 | dunameis rendering | "great powers" | "great works of power" | "extraordinary displays of power" | "great works of power" -- preserves dunamis echo from v.10 |
| 16 | Preposition: EIS vs. EN | "into the name" (EIS) | "in the name" (EN) | "into the name" (EIS) | "into the name" -- Sinaiticus reads EIS, confirmed in manuscript |
| 18 | "the Spirit" vs. "Holy Spirit" | "the Spirit" | "the Spirit" | "the Spirit" | "the Spirit" -- all three agree, following Sinaiticus |
| 20 | Curse formulation | "may-it-be unto destruction" | "May your silver perish with you" | "May your silver go to destruction with you" | "May your silver perish with you" -- LOGOS captures the optative force idiomatically |
| 21 | "word" vs. "matter" | "this word" (logos) | "this matter" | "this work" | "this matter" -- logos here means "matter/affair," not a spoken word |
| 23 | Hebraic genitives | "gall of bitterness ... bond of unrighteousness" | "gall of bitterness ... bond of unrighteousness" | "poisoned with bitterness ... shackled by sin" | Preserves Hebraic genitives with EMET/LOGOS; biblical allusions must be retained |
| 26 | "desert" referent | "This is a desert [place]" | "This is a desert road" | Integrated into sentence | "This is a desert road" -- follows LOGOS; the gloss likely describes the road |
| 27 | "powerful-one" vs. "court official" | "a powerful-one of Kandake" (dunastes) | "a court official of Candace" | "a court official of the Kandake" | "a court official of Candace" -- dunastes here means an official, not merely "powerful one" |
| 33 | "generation" vs. "descendants" | "the generation of him" | "His generation" | "his descendants" | "His generation" -- preserves the deliberate ambiguity of genea |
| 34 | Question phrasing | "Concerning himself, or concerning some other?" | "concerning himself or concerning someone else?" | "Himself, or someone else?" | "concerning himself or concerning someone else?" -- follows LOGOS/EMET's fuller form |
| 39 | Verb for Spirit's action | "snatched-away" | "snatched ... away" | "swept ... away" | "snatched ... away" -- herpasen means forceful seizure, not gentle sweeping |
| 39 | Eunuch's joy | "going on his way rejoicing" | "going on his way rejoicing" | "went on his way overflowing with joy" | "going on his way rejoicing" -- 2-to-1; the Greek is simply eporeueto chairon |

— SOPHIA
